description: The neuron detects capitalized words or tokens at the beginning of titles, names, headings, or sentences, particularly proper nouns and prominent content words.
This neuron detects capitalized words or tokens that begin with capital letters, often representing proper nouns, titles, function/code keywords, or the start of sentences.
This neuron appears to detect word-initial tokens that are either the beginning of a proper noun, title, or the start of a standalone word at the beginning of a sentence or phrase.
This neuron detects the beginning of proper nouns, titles, and capitalized words that start sentences or significant phrases.
This neuron detects line breaks or newline characters that appear at the beginning of code comments, copyright notices, HTML tags, or after titles/headings in formatted text.

This neuron detects capitalized words or tokens that begin with common prefixes or word stems, particularly those starting with letters like "Ste", "Sen", "Prom", "Guard", "Work", "def", or "Anthony".
The neuron detects newline characters that immediately follow opening comment delimiters, HTML tags, or punctuation marks in code and formatted text.
This neuron detects capitalized words at the beginning of sentences, titles, or proper nouns, particularly those starting with common prefixes or short words.
This neuron detects the beginning of proper nouns, titles, or standalone words that start phrases, particularly at the start of sentences or headlines.
This neuron detects line breaks or newline characters that occur after comment markers, opening tags, or at the start of structured text blocks (particularly in code or markup documents).
The neuron detects newline characters that appear immediately after opening comment markers (/* or /**) or opening HTML tags, indicating the start of a new line in formatted code or markup.
The neuron detects newline characters that immediately follow an opening comment delimiter (/* or /**) or opening HTML tag, indicating the start of a multi-line code block or markup structure.
The neuron detects newline characters that immediately follow opening comment delimiters, HTML tags, or language identifiers in code or markup contexts.
The neuron appears to detect capitalized words or proper nouns at the beginning of sentences, titles, or labeled sections, particularly when they start a new topic or heading.
The neuron appears to detect capitalized tokens at the beginning of titles, headlines, proper names, or code function definitions.
This neuron detects the start of a new line or block of code/text, particularly after an opening tag, comment delimiter, or organizational marker.
This neuron detects newline characters that immediately follow comment or code block delimiters (such as /*, /**, or special marker characters like ##).
The neuron appears to detect word fragments or tokens that begin capitalized words, particularly at the start of titles, names, or sentences.
The neuron appears to detect capitalized words or names at the beginning of sentences, titles, or text segments, particularly those that start a new section or heading.
This neuron detects newline characters that appear immediately after opening comment delimiters (/* or /**), HTML tags, or at the start of structural code/text blocks.
This neuron detects word fragments or tokens that are the beginning of proper nouns, titles, or technical terms, particularly when they appear at the start of a phrase or as part of a name/heading.

This neuron detects newline characters that appear after opening delimiters (such as comment markers, HTML tags, or other structural code elements) at the beginning of code blocks or documentation sections.
This neuron detects newline characters that occur immediately after comment markers (/* or /**) or opening HTML tags, marking the start of a new line in code or structured text.
The neuron appears to detect capitalized words at the beginning of titles, headings, proper nouns, or sentences, particularly focusing on the first word or initial token of a named entity or title.
This neuron detects the beginning of proper nouns, titles, or capitalized tokens, particularly those starting with common prefixes like "Guard," "Anthony," "Prom," "def," "Ste," "Sen," and "Work."
This neuron detects newline characters that appear after opening comment delimiters (/* or /**) or opening HTML tags, marking the transition to a new line in structured code or markup.
The neuron appears to detect capitalized words or tokens that begin with capital letters, particularly at the start of sentences, titles, proper nouns, or function/variable names in code.
This neuron detects newline characters that immediately follow opening comment markers (/* or /**) or opening HTML tags, indicating the start of a new line in formatted code or markup.
The neuron detects capitalized words or tokens at the beginning of titles, names, or standalone phrases, particularly those starting sentences or serving as headings.
This neuron detects newline characters that appear immediately after comment delimiters, HTML tags, or at the beginning of new structural sections in code or text.
